pattern: Since the smaller cube in any qualifying pair can be at most roughly the cube root of half the bound, enumerating every ordered pair of cube bases directly and tallying how many times each sum occurs finds every value with two or more representations.
hints: If a <= b and a^3 + b^3 <= n, then a can be at most around the cube root of n/2, which keeps the number of pairs to check small even for the largest bound.
For each starting base a, increase b from a upward, accumulating a^3 + b^3 into a running tally of how many (a, b) pairs produce that same sum, and stop as soon as the sum exceeds n.
After tallying every pair, keep only the sums that were produced by at least two distinct pairs, and return them sorted in ascending order.

edge_inputs.description: the classic taxicab number and a second good integer just above it
no good integers exist below this bound
a bound landing exactly on a good integer must include it
a bound one less than a good integer must exclude it
a very small bound where no pair of positive cubes can even be formed twice

stress.description: random bounds across the full constraint range
